_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for a plant cell. Image: plant-cell_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 1448x1086 landscape). Correctly shows all three plant-defining features (cell wall, large central vacuole, chloroplasts) that a prior animal-cell generation lacked -- that animal-cell PNG is earmarked separately, NOT reused here. To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the returned URL in `src`, add this block to the target biology page (Class 11 Cell: The Unit of Life). Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-15 -- all 9 placements confirmed live via full playthrough (100% accuracy).
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Cell wall
hotspots.detail: A rigid outer layer made of **cellulose** that gives the cell its shape and support — plant cells have this, animal cells don't.

hotspots.label: Cell membrane
hotspots.detail: A thin, selectively permeable layer just inside the cell wall, controlling what enters and leaves the cell.

hotspots.label: Large central vacuole
hotspots.detail: A large fluid-filled sac that maintains **turgor pressure** and stores water, ions, and waste — much bigger than in animal cells.

hotspots.label: Nucleus
hotspots.detail: The control centre of the cell, containing the DNA, enclosed by a nuclear envelope.

hotspots.label: Nucleolus
hotspots.detail: A dense region inside the nucleus where **ribosomal RNA** is made.

hotspots.label: Chloroplast
hotspots.detail: The site of **photosynthesis**, containing chlorophyll — the feature that makes plant cells green and lets them make their own food.

hotspots.label: Mitochondrion
hotspots.detail: The **powerhouse of the cell**, generating ATP through respiration.

hotspots.label: Endoplasmic reticulum
hotspots.detail: A network of membranes involved in protein and lipid synthesis and transport.

hotspots.label: Golgi apparatus
hotspots.detail: Modifies, packages, and ships proteins and lipids to their destinations.

generation_prompt: A clean anatomically accurate educational illustration of a plant cell, cut open to reveal its organelles, standard biology-textbook diagram, semi-realistic painterly style. Rounded-rectangular cell bounded by a thick cell wall with the cell membrane just inside; a large central vacuole occupying much of the middle; a prominent round nucleus (showing a nucleolus) to one side; several green oval chloroplasts; several bean-shaped mitochondria; folded ER near the nucleus; a Golgi apparatus stack; small dot-like ribosomes. Muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Landscape 4:3, high resolution.
